operating_state: Nike reported fiscal 2024 revenues of $51.4 billion, flat versus prior year with 1% growth currency-neutral, driven by Greater China growth offsetting North America decline and significant Converse weakness. NIKE Brand revenues increased 1% with footwear growth despite unit declines as strategic pricing actions contributed 3 percentage points of growth. NIKE Direct revenues grew 1% with comparable store sales growth offsetting digital sales decline from reduced traffic.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded 110 basis points to 44.6% primarily driven by strategic pricing actions contributing approximately 200 basis points, partially offset by unfavorable foreign exchange impacts and higher product input costs. Selling and administrative expenses increased 1% with higher demand creation spending for advertising and sports marketing while operating overhead remained flat. The company executed restructuring charges for enterprise-wide workforce reduction and organizational streamlining.
material_events: Nike completed multi-year enterprise initiative targeting cost savings through product assortment simplification, increased automation, organizational streamlining and scale leverage. The company returned $7.1 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends under its four-year authorization. Inventories decreased 11% as the company proactively managed supply levels and reduced purchase obligations.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency headwinds reduced revenues by approximately $141 million and created 40 basis points unfavorable gross margin impact from weakening Chinese yuan and European currencies. Consumer spending remained cautious amid global economic uncertainty and elevated promotional activity across the athletic industry. Geopolitical tensions in Greater China continued pressuring brand momentum while inflationary pressures from higher product input costs negatively impacted margins despite strategic pricing offsets.
forward_outlook: Management expects fiscal 2025 revenue to decline low-single digits as the company navigates marketplace dynamics and implements its next phase of innovation strategy while reinvesting majority of annual wage savings from restructuring to fuel growth initiatives. The company continues investing in global Enterprise Resource Planning Platform, data analytics, demand sensing and end-to-end technology foundation alongside digital capabilities and direct-to-consumer channels.
uncertainty_profile: Operating environment volatility expected to continue with potential material adverse impacts from macroeconomic and geopolitical conditions, particularly ongoing regulatory scrutiny regarding labor practices and potential trade policy changes affecting manufacturing costs. Multiple securities class action and shareholder derivative lawsuits are ongoing related to stock price volatility, potentially resulting in substantial costs and management distraction. Supply chain risks persist from contractor compliance failures, manufacturing disruptions, and transportation delays affecting product availability and margins.
